_comment: Connector-specific edits a cli-printing-press reprint or onboard --resume must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and FAILS CI if a reprint dropped one.

handfixes.summary: manifest.json name/entry_point/command use the clean `afi-mcp`, not the press `-pp-mcp`
handfixes.why: onboard re-copies the SOURCE manifest each --resume and the press source keeps `-pp-mcp`. The repo ships `afi-mcp`: the Makefile builds `bin/afi-mcp` and server.json's package identifier is `afi-mcp.mcpb`, so an `-pp-mcp` entry_point points at a binary that is never built (the MCP server fails to launch). No mechanical gate catches this (the build name and the manifest name are checked independently); the 4.24.0 re-vendor silently reverted the correct 0.1.0 value and CodeRabbit flagged it Critical on PR #100. See project_onboard_manifest_deleak_and_envvar.

handfixes.summary: channel_workflow.go `workflow archive` syncs the spec-derived `applications` resource, not an empty list
handfixes.why: The 4.24.0 reprint emitted `resources := []string{}` (empty) where afi 0.1.0 carried `[]string{"applications"}` and the sibling 4.24 connectors carry their own populated lists (acronis: clients/task-manager/..., connectwise-control: session_groups). An empty list makes `afi-cli workflow archive` a silent no-op (the registered command builds and runs but syncs nothing). No mechanical gate catches it; CodeRabbit flagged it Major on PR #100. A re-vendor re-emits the empty list. See project_afi_onboard_update_424_traps; the press's compound-workflow resource inference dropping afi's list is a printing-press retro candidate.
